_publications._publicationTitle: Transplanting neurofibromatosis-1 gene knockout neural stem cells improve functional recovery in rats with spinal cord injury by enhancing the mTORC2 pathway.

_contextSnippet: All rats subjected to SCI had a score of zero on the first day after the injury, which indicates the success of SCI. All injured rats achieved higher BBB scores from the 1st week to the 8th week, and the rats transplanted with the LV-NF-1-treated NSCs achieved notably better BBB scores
details: All rats subjected to SCI had BBB score of zero on first day after injury, indicating successful SCI model. Rats transplanted with LV-NF-1-treated NSCs achieved notably better BBB scores than other injured groups from 2nd week through 8th week
